narrative: OMX30 enters Friday supported by overnight US tech strength (NASDAQ +1.30%) and Asian recovery (Nikkei +1.20%), with correlation signal turning modestly bullish at +0.327%. However, technical structure remains choppy: ADX at 0.6 (well below 18) signals weak trend definition, MACD sits below signal line, and RSI 48.13 is neutral—reducing confidence to Low. Yields fell 66bps overnight, pressuring bank NIM assumptions and dampening the typical summer risk-on narrative. USD/SEK weakness (-0.33%) and EUR/SEK decline (-0.25%) work against major exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson). Copper's +0.76% pop and gold's modest -0.50% dip create mixed commodity signals. The index sits 7% above its 200-MA (bullish structure) but only 1.5% above 50-MA, with OMX30 52W range at 90% utilization and -2.2% from recent highs—positioning suggests consolidation and mean-reversion risk.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens with light bullish bias from overnight US/Asia rallies, but weak ADX (0.6) and neutral technicals (RSI 48, MACD below signal) cap conviction. Support at 3085 (lower Bollinger), pivot at ~3123, resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Expect range trade 3100–3150 unless US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET spark fresh momentum. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken, Swedbank) face headwind from 66bps yield decline overnight—monitor NIM repricing. Exporters (Volvo B, Atlas Copco, Ericsson B) struggle with weak USD/SEK and EUR/SEK; watch for stabilization. Boliden outperforms on copper +0.76% despite gold weakness. VIX 16.1 reflects mild risk-off; watch for any escalation into weekend. Avoid overextension; size accordingly given weak trend definition.
drivers: Positive US overnight (NASDAQ +1.30%, S&P +0.81%) and Asia (Nikkei +1.20%) support risk appetite
Copper +0.76% benefits Boliden; gold -0.50% mild headwind for miners
OMX30 well-supported above 200-MA (+7.0%) but ADX 0.6 signals weak trend conviction; MACD below signal and RSI 48 neutral limit upside
Yields down (-0.66% on US 10Y) compress bank NIM expansion; USD/SEK weak (-0.33%) headwind for exporters
VIX uptick to 16.1 and 52W position (-2.2% from high) suggest market consolidation rather than breakout

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield down 66bps overnight compresses NIM expansion expectations. Swedish mortgage dynamics already sensitive to rate cuts; weakness limits upside.
USD/SEK -0.33% and EUR/SEK -0.25% reduce export competitiveness. Weak ADX limits risk-on sentiment needed to offset FX drag.
NASDAQ +1.30% overnight is supportive, but weak ADX and MACD below signal limit momentum. USD/SEK weakness also headwind.
Copper +0.76% is direct revenue driver; gold -0.50% is minor offset. Boliden outperforms on commodity upside despite weak index technicals.
EUR/SEK weakness (-0.25%) increases sourcing costs, but weak trend limits broader consumer rotation.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.76% drives upside; gold -0.50% minor offset. Only OMX30 stock with clear directional tailwind.
Industrials heavyweight faces USD/SEK headwind (-0.33%); weak ADX limits reversal opportunity.
Bank flagship underperforms on 10Y yield collapse (-66bps); NIM repricing risk into next earnings.
NASDAQ +1.30% overnight is positive signal, but USD/SEK weakness and weak OMX30 trend limit follow-through.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is key event risk for afternoon session. No major Swedish economic data today. Watch for any repricing of Fed expectations if US labor data surprises.
topRisk: ADX weakness (0.6) means trend confirmation is fragile; break below 3085 could trigger capitulation into 3050s. Conversely, any yield stabilization or USD strength rally could spark sharp reversion higher. Weekend geopolitical risk (Trump DoJ appointment, trade tensions) could gap opening Monday.
technicalSummary: Above 200-MA (+7.0%) but only +1.5% above 50-MA — narrow margin of safety
MACD below signal line, histogram -3.074 — no momentum confirmation
RSI 48.13 perfectly neutral; ADX 0.6 critically weak — reduce confidence per rules to Low
Stochastic K=37, D=31.4 neutral; no overbought/oversold condition
Bollinger %B=0.51 mid-range; width 4.3% shows modest volatility; upper half support
52W at 90% range utilization, -2.2% from high — consolidation zone, mean reversion risk
